manipulated_prompt_obj.content:
Provide one single-word clue that relates to some of your team's words from the list below. You can choose to how many and to which words your clue relates to.
The goal is to have your partner guess all of your team's words based on the clues you give them, before the other team has uncovered all of theirs. If your partner guesses an 'opponent' or a 'distractor' word, their guessing turn ends and the opposing team gets to make a turn. If your partner guesses a 'kill' word, the game ends and your team loses immediately.
The more words your clue relates to, the quicker you can win the game, but the harder the associations may be to guess for your partner, so choose your clue word wisely. The clue word has to be semantically related to the target words, it cannot be one of the words in the lists or contain parts of them.

Always give your single-word clue and your comma-separated list of related target words in the following format and make your answers as short as possible, never include any other text than is required in this form:

CLUE: <WORD>
TARGETS: <TARGETS>

Your team words are: godmother, nephew, allergy, ordinance, oven, hypocrite, geisha, vigilante, storeroom.
Your opponent's team words are: libido, blazer, bedroom, mutt, tanker, ledger, seminary, vandalism.
Distractor words are: riddance, sorcerer, spider, checkup, teacher, ruse, courtroom.
The kill words are: census.

message.content:
CLUE: Kitchen
TARGETS: oven, storeroom, allergy
message.reasoning:
We need give a clue (single word) that relates to some of our team words, without being any of the listed words or containing parts. Must not be a word that appears as any of the listed words. Provide clue and list of target words we intend partner to guess.

We want to maximize number perhaps but ensure not risky with opponent/distractor/kill.

Our team words: godmother, nephew, allergy, ordinance (maybe ordinance is law), oven, hypocrite, geisha, vigilante, storeroage (storeroom).

We need a clue that can link to multiple.

Potential clue: "Japan"? Relates to geisha, maybe godmother? No. Nephew? No. Might be too specific.

Clue: "Fire"? Could relate to oven (fire), vigilante (firefighter? Not exactly), maybe hypocrite (fire? no). Not good.

Clue: "Recipe"? Oven, maybe storeroom (pantry), not others.

Clue: "Family"? godmother, nephew, maybe hyp? Not.

Clue: "Law"? ordinance relates, vigilante opposite of law, maybe hypocrite? Not.

Clue: "Secret"? godmother (godmother may be secret?), hypocrite, vigilante (secret identity). Might be ambiguous.

Need to consider opponent words: libido, blazer, bedroom, mutt, tanker, ledger, seminary, vandalism. Distractor: riddance, sorcerer, spider, checkup, teacher, ruse, courtroom. Kill: census.

Our clue must not be any of those.

Potential clue "Kitchen"? Relates to oven, storeroom (pantry), maybe allergy (food allergies), maybe godmother? Not. Could be 3.

Clue "Food"? oven, allergy, storeroom, maybe nephew (kids love food), but ambiguous but okay.

But risk of opponent words? None of opponent words directly food.

Maybe safer to choose 2-3 targets.

Clue "Pantry"? Relates to storeroom, maybe oven (kitchen). But pantry is specific to storage not oven. Could be okay.

But must be single word.

Clue "Storage" maybe relate to storeroom, oven (stores heat). Not.

Maybe choose "Japanese": geisha, maybe godmother? Not.

Could do "Justice": vigilante, ordinance (law), hypocrite (lack justice). Might be good: three targets: vigilante, ordinance, hypocrite? Hypocrite maybe about justice? Might be ambiguous.

Better "Law": ordinance, vigilante (outside law), hypocrite (not following law). Could be 3.

But opponent word "ledger" also law/finance. Might confuse.

Maybe we want clue "Kitchen": oven, storeroom (pantry), allergy (food). That's 3. No opponent/distractor meaning kitchen.

Check distractor "courtroom" includes room but not kitchen. Might still be okay.

Thus:

CLUE: Kitchen
TARGETS: oven, storeroom, allergy

Make sure "kitchen" is not in any list: not present.

Return format.
